raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "A bill establishing a statewide guaranteed income program for homeless students in California was advanced by the Senate Education Committee.",
    "The proposed program would give homeless high school seniors $1,000 a month for five months.",
    "Guaranteed income programs have been shown to help people lift themselves out of poverty when provided with financial tools.",
    "Approximately 270,000 students in California's K-12 school system are experiencing homelessness, having grown by nearly half over the past decade.",
    "A recent survey found that more than 90 percent of unhoused youth respondents described a career goal requiring education beyond high school.",
    "Only 16 percent of unhoused youth respondents believed they could attend or graduate from college within the next five years.",
    "The Success, Opportunity, & Academic Resilience (SOAR) program would provide guaranteed income for 12th grade unhoused students lacking a fixed nighttime residence.",
    "Senator Cortese helped create Santa Clara County’s first-in-the-nation universal basic income program for transition-age foster youth.",
    "The Santa Clara County pilot program was scaled statewide under Senator Cortese’s SB 739 as part of California's budget."
  ]
}
```
